metadata.feature_description: Agentic Filtering Framework using MCP tools and LlamaIndex to allow natural language IFC data audits and visualization queries. Users describe filter intent in plain language; a LlamaIndex agent orchestrates MCP tools (get_project_schema, create_filter_set, add_filter_condition, apply_filter_set_to_context) to construct and apply filter sets, with a Svelte chat UI and SSE-based state sync.

state.current_objective: Execute the 15-step implementation plan for the Agentic Filtering Framework.

execution_log.step: Define JSON-RPC schemas for four MCP tools and create tool registry module (step-01)
Implement get_project_schema tool returning IFC classes, attributes, operators, relationships (step-02)
Implement create_filter_set MCP tool wrapping existing DB function (step-03)
Implement add_filter_condition MCP tool appending conditions to filter sets (step-04)
Implement apply_filter_set_to_context MCP tool with SSE event emission (step-05)
Create LlamaIndex agent workflow with Discovery/Validation/Creation/Application steps (step-06)
Create POST /agent/chat FastAPI endpoint with SSE streaming (step-07)
Create GET /stream/agent-events SSE endpoint for filter-applied notifications (step-08)
Build Svelte ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ToolCallBadge components (step-09)
Build Svelte ModelConfig panel with localStorage persistence (step-10)
Wire agent-events SSE to search state for view refresh on filter application (step-11)
Create /agent route or integrate chat into main sidebar (step-12)
Add pytest tests for MCP tools and agent workflow (step-13)
End-to-end integration test: NL query → agent → filter → application → refresh (step-14)
Run full test suite, linting, frontend type checks, and validate E2E scenario (step-15)

handoff_notes: Plan complete. All 15 steps are defined with file targets, actions, and constraints. The Execution Agent should start with step-01 (MCP tool schemas) and proceed sequentially. Backend steps (01-08) can be developed before frontend steps (09-12). Tests (13-15) should run after implementation. Key constraint: LLM API keys are per-request from frontend, never stored server-side.
